DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS' UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT SF GARAGE COMPANY, INC. TO PROVIDE FURTHER ANSWERS TO FIRST SET OF INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SF GARAGE DID NOT PROVIDE A VERIFICATION OF ITS RESPONSES; ACCORDINGLY, ALL OBJECTIONS TO THE INTERROGATORIES, INCLUDING OBJECTIONS BASED ON PRIVILEGE OR ON THE PROTECTION FOR WORK PRODUCT, ARE WAIVED (SUBJECT TO THE PROVISIONS OF CCP 2030.290(A) PROVIDING FOR RELIEF FROM SUCH WAIVER). IN ADDITION, DEFENDANT SF GARAGE HAS NOT PROVIDED ADEQUATE RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1, IN THAT IS HAS FAILED TO IDENTIFY FACTS ON WHICH IT BASES ITS DENIAL OF REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS AND IDENTIFY SUPPORTING DOCUMENTS AND KNOWLEDGEABLE WITNESSES. DEFENDANT SF GARAGE SHALL PROVIDE AMENDED RESPONSES WITHIN 14 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM ROGER MEAD, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS' UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL A FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST SET OF REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION TO DEFENDANT SF GARAGE COMPANY, INC., AND TO COMPEL FURTHER PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SF GARAGE DID NOT PROVIDE A VERIFICATION OF ITS RESPONSES; ACCORDINGLY, ALL OBJECTIONS TO THE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION, INCLUDING OBJECTIONS BASED ON PRIVILEGE OR ON THE PROTECTION FOR WORK PRODUCT, ARE WAIVED (SUBJECT TO THE PROVISIONS OF CCP 2031.300(A) PROVIDING FOR RELIEF FROM SUCH WAIVER). IN ADDITION, IT APPEARS THAT DEFENDANT SF GARAGE HAS NOT PRODUCED DOCUMENTS IN CONFORMANCE WITH THE STATEMENTS OF COMPLIANCE IN IT (UNVERIFIED) RESPONSES. DEFENDANT SF GARAGE SHALL, WITHIN 14 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER, PROVIDE VERIFIED RESPONSES, AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS IN CONFORMANCE WITH ITS STATEMENTS OF COMPLIANCE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM ROGER MEAD, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

DEFENDANT SF GARAGE COMPANY INCS OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SUBMITTED IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 63156953) FILED BY DEFENDANT SF GARAGE COMPANY, INC.

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT SF GARAGE COMPANY, INC.'S NOTICE OF DEFENDANT SF GARAGE COMPANY INCS MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT WITH PLAINTIFFS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. SUR-REPLY IS STRICKEN, AS IT AS FILED WITHOUT THE COURT'S PERMISSION. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT WAS NOT TIMELY SUBMITTED, TO THE DETRIMENT OF OPPOSING PARTIES. DEFENDANT SF GARAGE COMPANY, INC. FAILED TO PROVIDE SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE THAT THE TECH-BILT FACTORS WERE MET IN THIS CASE: (1) A ROUGH APPROXIMATION OF P'S TOTAL RECOVERY AND THE SETTLOR'S PROPORTIONATE LIABILITY; (2) THE AMOUNT PAID IN SETTLEMENT; (3) THE ALLOCATION OF SETTLEMENT PROCEEDS AMONG PLAINTIFFS; (4)THE RECOGNITION THAT A SETTLOR SHOULD PAY LESS IN A SETTLEMENT THAN HE WOULD IF HE WERE FOUND LIABLE AT TRIAL; (5) THE FINANCIAL CONDITIONS AND INSURANCE LIMITS OF SETTLING DEFENDANTS; AND, (6) THE EXISTENCE OF FRAUD, COLLUSION OR TORTIOUS CONDUCT AIMED TO INJURE THE INTEREST OF NONSETTLING DEFENDANTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, NOT REPORTED (D501)

Notice Of Defendant Sf Garage Company Incs Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement With Plaintiffs
Off Calendar May-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ray Bacerdo (510-596-0888) for Plaintiff/ opposing party John David Leshy.
Matthew Giampaili (925-408-9575) for Defendant/Moving party SF GARAGE COMPANY, INC..

Hearing is held. Argued re: clarification, substantial evidence, settlement. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SF GARAGE COMPANY, INC.'S Notice Of Defendant Sf Garage Company Incs Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement With Plaintiffs is DENIED without prejudice. Sur-Reply is stricken, as it as filed without the Court's permission. The Court notes that the settlement agreement was not timely submitted, to the detriment of opposing parties. Defendant SF Garage Company, Inc. failed to provide substantial evidence that the Tech-Bilt factors were met in this case:
(1) A rough approximation of P's total recovery and the settlor's proportionate liability;
(2) The amount paid in settlement;
(3) The allocation of settlement proceeds among plaintiffs;
(4)The recognition that a settlor should pay less in a settlement than he would if he were found liable at trial;
(5) The financial conditions and insurance limits of settling defendants; and,
(6) the existence of fraud, collusion or tortious conduct aimed to injure the interest of nonsettling defendants. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)
